meaning: adj.职责所在的,负有...义务的
examples: She felt it was incumbent on herself to act immediately.
Incumbent officeholders are difficult to defeat at the polls.
The tight race is almost a duplicate of the elections in Georgia and South Dakota last month that pitted a Republican challenger against a Democratic incumbent.
If they win now, they will have a voter-tested incumbent running for a full term in 2014.
Too often boards retreat into complacency and dependency after years of strong leadership by the incumbent.
